name: Abdullah ibn Afif
title: Blind Lion
shortDescription: The blind old man and former warrior who roared against Yazid and Ibn Ziyad in the Kufa mosque and was martyred.
fullStory:
Abdullah ibn Afif al-Azdi was a loyal Shia of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who had lost his eyes in the path of Islam during the battles of Jamal and Siffin. He was a blind old man who spent his days worshiping in the Kufa mosque.

After the event of Ashura, Ibn Ziyad went up to the pulpit in the Kufa mosque and audaciously said: 'Praise be to God who made Yazid victorious and killed the liar son of a liar (Imam Hussain)!'

Amidst the deadly and fearful silence of the Kufans, suddenly the voice of this blind old man sounded like thunder: 'O son of Marjana! The liar son of a liar is you, your father, and the one who put you in power! You kill the Prophet's children and speak on the pulpit of Muslims?'

Ibn Ziyad, exposed, ordered his arrest. Abdullah escaped with his tribe's help, but at night, agents raided his house. Guided by his daughter, he fought with a sword until he was captured and, by Ibn Ziyad's order, hanged in Kufa.
quoteArabic: يا ابْنَ مَرْجانَةَ! إِنَّ الكَذّابَ ابْنَ الكَذّابِ أَنْتَ وَأَبُوكَ...
quote: (His cry in the mosque:) O son of Marjana! The liar son of a liar is you, your father, and the one who appointed you...
